title:
iTero
Scan Experience
subtitle: Redesigning the clinical scanning workflow to improve speed, clarity, and decision-making in real treatment conditions.

slides.title:
iTero
Scan Experience
slides.description: Redesigning a fragmented clinical workflow to improve speed, clarity, and confidence during real-time scanning.

image.caption: iTero scanner during live clinical use

slides.label: Context
slides.title: A continuous clinical flow
slides.content: The iTero experience spans setup, scanning, and validation. Each phase serves a different purpose but must work as one system under time pressure.

image.caption: Full RX → Scan → View workflow overview

slides.label: Core Problems
slides.title: Where the workflow breaks down
slides.description: Each phase evolved independently, creating friction in moments that require speed and confidence.

issues.title: Rigid setup flow (RX)
issues.description: Users must complete all fields before starting, creating hesitation

issues.title: Unstable scanning interaction
issues.description: Tools are scattered and interrupt real-time flow

issues.title: Tools require interpretation
issues.description: Outputs are unclear and require repeated adjustments

issues.title: Unclear validation state
issues.description: No clear 'done' moment, causing repeated checking

slides.label: User Research
slides.title: What actually happens
slides.content: Observations and interviews revealed real friction in behavior, not just interface issues.

quotes.text: When I open a case, I’m not always sure which procedure to choose. I go back and forth before starting.
quotes.author: Dr. Yael Levi

quotes.text: I double-check everything before I scan. If something feels off, I stop.
quotes.author: Dr. Amir Cohen

quotes.text: If I need to look for tools during scanning, I lose the flow immediately.
quotes.author: Dr. Noa Ben-David

quotes.text: Some tools don’t clearly tell me if it’s good or not. I just try again to be safe.
quotes.author: Dr. Eyal Mizrahi

quotes.text: I finish scanning but still rotate and check again. I’m not always sure I’m done.
quotes.author: Dr. Daniel Katz

quotes.text: Sometimes I adjust things even if they look fine, just because I don’t fully trust the result.
quotes.author: Dr. Lior Haddad

goals.title: Increase speed
goals.description: Shorten time from RX to completed scan

slides.label: RX — Case Setup
slides.title: From rigid prescription to flexible entry
slides.beforeDescription: RX (Prescription) is where clinicians define treatment before scanning. Previously, it was rigid, text-heavy, and required full completion before starting.
slides.afterDescription: The redesigned RX simplifies setup into a flexible entry point, allowing faster start with better clarity.
beforeBullets: Must complete all fields before scanning
slides.highlight: Users start scanning faster with less hesitation.

slides.label: Icon System
slides.title: From visual noise to a scalable language
slides.beforeDescription: The icon set evolved over time without a shared system. Different styles, stroke weights, and metaphors created visual noise and slowed recognition during critical moments.
slides.afterDescription: A unified icon system was created based on a consistent grid, stroke rules, and simplified metaphors — improving clarity, scalability, and speed of recognition.
beforeBullets: Did not scale with new features
slides.highlight: Icons became predictable, reducing hesitation during real-time scanning.

slides.label: Scan — Real-time Interaction
slides.title: From fragmented actions to continuous flow
slides.beforeDescription: During scanning, clinicians had to split attention between the patient, the model, and the interface. Tools were scattered and lacked hierarchy, forcing users to search and think in moments that require continuous motion.
slides.afterDescription: The scanning experience was redesigned around a unified interaction layer, where tools are organized by priority and placed in a consistent location — allowing clinicians to act without breaking flow.
slides.highlight: Scanning shifted from stop-and-think interaction to continuous, flow-based operation.

slides.label: Scan
slides.title: Tabs for flexibility
slides.content: Tabs allow switching between scan contexts, giving doctors more control without breaking flow.

slides.label: Tools
slides.title: From control to decision
slides.beforeDescription: Tools required interpretation and repeated adjustments.
slides.afterDescription: Tools provide clear feedback and simplified actions.

slides.label: View
slides.title: Clear validation state
slides.beforeDescription: No clear completion state, causing repeated checks.
slides.highlight: Users know when they are done.

slides.label: View
slides.title: Multi-layer comparison
slides.content: A multi-layer panel allows comparing scans with opacity control, making differences immediately visible.

slides.label: Prototyping & Testing
slides.title: Built with Cursor
slides.content: All flows were prototyped and tested using Cursor, enabling rapid iteration and realistic simulations of the workflow.
